steps.title: Evaluate f(-4) by substituting into the absolute value expression
steps.content:
Start with $f(x) = 3 + |-2x - x^2|$. Substitute $x = -4$:
$$f(-4) = 3 + |-2(-4) - (-4)^2|$$
Simplify inside the absolute value:
$$-2(-4) - (-4)^2 = 8 - 16 = -8$$
Take the absolute value:
$$|-8| = 8$$
Therefore $f(-4) = 3 + 8 = 11$, so $c = 11$.

steps.title: Evaluate g(11) by substituting the value of c
steps.content:
Now use $g(w) = |\frac{-w}{w-1}| - w + 5$ with $w = c = 11$:
$$g(11) = \left|\frac{-11}{11-1}\right| - 11 + 5$$
Simplify the fraction:
$$\frac{-11}{10} = -1.1$$
Take the absolute value:
$$\left|-1.1\right| = 1.1$$
Complete the calculation:
$$g(11) = 1.1 - 11 + 5 = 1.1 - 6 = -4.9$$
